hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to bulky aromatic residues (e.g., W, F, Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5/ECL2 complementarity while disrupting off-target (SSTR3/5) interactions. Mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (e.g., L, I, V) will stabilize the peptide in the SSTR2-specific ECL3/TM6 pocket and reduce off-target binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The campaign is not yet achieving consistent selectivity over native SST-14. Structural failures dominate, and the low pass rate suggests poor design quality. A focused mutation strategy at non-pharmacophore positions is needed to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Focus on non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target contacts while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14).
Introduce mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to break conserved interactions with off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining SSTR2 binding.
